description_long: This sculpture is a carved stone relief depicting three standing human figures, likely of ancient Egyptian origin. The central figure is a male wearing a tall, conical headdress and a kilt, flanked by two female figures each adorned with distinctive headdresses, one of which features a sun disk and cow horns. All three figures are rendered in a highly stylized, formal pose, with arms at their sides and one leg forward, typical of ancient Egyptian statuary. The figures are attached to a rectangular stone backing, which is inscribed with hieroglyphic text at the base. The surface is smooth but shows subtle textural variations, suggesting it is carved from a single block of stone. The overall coloration is a uniform light beige, consistent with limestone or similar material.
description: A carved stone relief of three ancient Egyptian figures, with detailed headdresses and hieroglyphic inscriptions at the base. The sculpture is rendered in a light beige stone with a smooth, slightly textured surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three figures in full detail, including their headdresses and the hieroglyphic inscriptions at the base.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the relief and the profile of the figures, especially the central male figure's tall headdress.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat, unadorned back of the stone slab, with no visible figures or inscriptions.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the profile of the figures and the thickness of the stone backing.
attribute_subject_matter: Three ancient Egyptian figures in formal attire with hieroglyphic inscriptions
attribute_location_context: No visible context; the background is plain and does not indicate a specific setting.
